lesson.menu_desc: Make offers and trade-offs the professional way -- with the second conditional (If we had more time, we would test it) -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 18 -- Negotiating Solutions -- making offers and trade-offs with the second conditional
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Andreia Heins | Aula 18 -- Negotiating Solutions

listenings.text: Hi Andreia, David Lewis here. Before your call with the supplier, let me brief you on the negotiation. We want a fair deal on the replacement console, and the terms are still open. Here is our position. The supplier wants full payment now, but we want to pay in two parts. So think in trade-offs. If they gave us thirty days to pay, we would sign today. If we paid the full amount up front, we would expect a longer warranty. Be flexible, but protect two things: the training date and the budget. If they offered free installation, we would accept a slightly higher price. Listen for their counteroffer, and look for a mutual win, something good for both sides. Do not say yes to the first number. Make one concession, ask for one in return, and aim for an agreement everyone can live with. Call me after the meeting. Thank you, Andreia.

Good morning. Thank you for meeting me to discuss the console. I would like to find a fair deal for both sides, so let me be open about our position. The terms are still flexible, but two things matter most to us: the training date and the budget. Here is my thinking. If you gave us thirty days to pay, we would sign the contract today. I understand you prefer full payment, so let me offer a trade-off. If we paid the full amount up front, we would ask for a longer warranty and free installation. That feels like a mutual win. I am ready to make one concession on the delivery window if you make one on price. If we agreed on those terms, we would both gain: you get a fast decision, and we protect our schedule. Shall we draft an agreement along these lines? I am confident we can reach a deal today. Thank you for your flexibility.

extra_audio.text: Let me describe how Andreia negotiates the deal. First, she states her position: the terms are flexible, but the training date and the budget matter most. Then she makes an offer with the second conditional: if you gave us thirty days, we would sign today. Next, she proposes a trade-off: if we paid up front, we would ask for a longer warranty. After that, she offers one concession and asks for one in return. Finally, she aims for a mutual agreement that works for both sides.
